When a small group of organisms (a small population) becomes separated from its original main population (for example, by crossing mountains and rivers, or by reaching a new island that makes it impossible to return easily), it usually finds itself in an environment different from the one it was in before.

This new environment has different resources and different competitors, so the new population will need different traits or adaptive abilities to become a strong competitor, rather than the ones they needed before.
The original population hasn't changed at all; it still needs to adapt to the environment just as before.
Over time, the new population begins to adapt to the new environment, and it starts to become less and less similar to the other populations.
After thousands or even millions of years, these two populations will eventually look so different that they can no longer be considered the same species.
We call this process “speciation,” that is, the formation of new species. Speciation is an inevitable result of evolution and also an extremely important part of it.

Technological determinism is a term that in practice encompasses many kinds of ideas, including the theory of technology push, technological necessity, and a strict definition—that human destiny is driven by the underlying logic associated with scientific laws and its manifestation in technology.

Most interpretations of technological determinism include these two general ideas: first, that the development of technology itself largely transcends cultural or political influences; and second, that technology in turn exerts an inherent “impact” on society that is not constrained by social conditions.
For example, someone might say that automobiles will inevitably promote the development of roads.
However, building a nationwide road network for only a small number of cars was not economically viable, so new modes of production emerged to reduce the cost of car ownership.

A high level of car ownership among the general public will also lead to a higher rate of road traffic accidents, which in turn will drive the invention of new medical technologies for repairing the injuries of the wounded.

Moreover, if this cannot be achieved, the consequences will be severe: stunted growth, malnutrition, and ultimately death.
The most readily available plant resources were probably leaves and the proteins found in legumes, but for primates like us, that kind of protein is hard to digest unless it’s cooked.
By contrast, animal foods (ants, termites, eggs) are not only easy to digest, but also provide large amounts of protein containing all the essential amino acids.
In short, if the way our ancestors solved the “protein problem” was no different from the method used by chimpanzees in today’s tropical savannas, we should not be surprised.
Sleep interruption refers to the process of intentionally waking up during normal sleep and then falling asleep again after a short period of time (10–60 minutes).
This is very easy to do: use a relatively quiet alarm to wake yourself without being fully awakened.
If you find yourself resetting the alarm in your sleep, place it on the other side of the room to force yourself out of bed to turn it off.
Other biologically based options include drinking plenty of fluids before bed (especially water or tea—both known diuretics), forcing yourself to get up to urinate.

To date, shipping is the most effective way to transport large numbers of people and goods across oceans.

Ancient civilizations and tribes began to domesticate them, consuming and using their milk, meat, hair, and hides.

Sleepers were introduced very early on to secure the rails. However, people gradually realized that if iron plates were placed on the rails, the rails would be more stable and effective.
This became common practice, but the iron plates caused greater wear on the wooden wagon wheels.
Eventually, wooden wheels were replaced by iron wheels. In 1767, the first all-iron track was introduced.

The handwritten draft signed by President John Hancock and Secretary Charles Thomson was approved by the Continental Congress on July 4, after which it was sent to John Dunlap’s print shop a few blocks away for printing.
Overnight, 150 to 200 copies were printed—the edition now widely known as the “Dunlap broadsides.”
On July 8, John Nixon publicly read this document for the first time in the yard of Independence Hall.
One of the letters was sent to George Washington on July 6, and on July 9 in New York, Washington read the letter aloud to his troops. A copy arrived in London on August 10.
The 25 surviving Dunlap broadsides are the earliest existing version of this document. The handwritten original has not been preserved.

Traffic flow studies the movement of drivers and vehicles between two points, as well as the interactions between them.

Fortunately, drivers' behavior often falls within a reasonably consistent range; therefore, traffic flow tends to exhibit a certain reasonable consistency and can be roughly represented mathematically.
To better represent traffic flow, relationships were established among the following three primary characteristics: (1) flow, (2) density, and (3) speed.
These relationships are useful for the planning, design, and operation of road facilities.

The Sundarbans cover an area of 3,850 square kilometers, about one-third of which is water/swamp land.

Snowfall, blowing snow, and water droplets or ice on the car windows can also obstruct visibility.
On the other hand, snowy and icy weather is very common in many countries, and transportation is hardly ever disrupted throughout the year.
A safari may be Africa’s most appealing tourist activity, and a highlight of many travelers’ itineraries.
The term “safari,” in its most common usage, refers to overland travel to դիտ astonishing African wildlife, especially on the savanna.
Some animals (such as elephants and giraffes) often like to come close to the vehicle, and standard equipment allows for better viewing.
Lions, cheetahs, and leopards are sometimes very wary of people, and with binoculars you can see them more clearly.
Hiking (also called “bush walking,” “trekking,” or “rambling”) mainly involves walking on foot and usually takes a few hours or lasts for several days.
The Paralympic Games will be held from August 24 to September 5, 2021. Some events will take place elsewhere in Japan.
Tokyo will become the only Asian city to have hosted the Summer Olympics twice, the first time in 1964.
If you booked flights and accommodation for 2020 before the postponement was announced, you may face a tricky situation.

Cancellation policies vary, but as of the end of March, most cancellation policies introduced because of the coronavirus had not been extended through July 2020—the Olympics’ originally scheduled dates.
Ticket prices for most events are expected to range from 2,500 yen to 130,000 yen, with the typical ticket price being around 7,000 yen.
Ironing damp clothes can help them dry. Even if the room doesn’t have an iron and ironing board, many hotels offer them on loan.
If there’s no iron, or you don’t like wearing ironed socks, you can try using a hair dryer (if there is one).
Be careful not to let the clothes get too hot (this may cause shrinking and, in extreme cases, scorch them).
There are many ways to purify water, and some are more effective against certain types of contamination.
In some areas, boiling water for one minute is enough, while in others it takes several minutes.
Filters vary in effectiveness. If you have concerns about this, you should consider buying sealed bottled water produced by a reputable company.
Travelers may encounter harmful animals that are uncommon in their home region.
Pests can damage food, cause irritation, and in more serious cases trigger allergic reactions or spread venom or infectious diseases.
Infectious diseases themselves, or dangerous animals capable of violently injuring or killing humans, are generally not classified as pests.
Duty-free shopping refers to the opportunity to buy duty-free goods at certain locations.
Travelers heading to countries or regions with high taxes can sometimes save a lot of money, and in such places it is especially worthwhile to buy products like alcohol and tobacco.
On the Buffalo–Pittsburgh highway, the most challenging driving conditions are on the stretch between Point Marion and Fairmont, which frequently passes through multiple remote and isolated areas.
If you're not used to driving on rural roads, stay sharp: this area is full of steep grades, narrow lanes, and sharp turns.

The posted speed limit here is noticeably lower than on the stretches before and after it; the common limit is 35–40 mph (56–64 km/h), and strictly observing the speed limit is even more important here than on other sections.
However, oddly enough, cell service here is much stronger than on many other routes along the way, such as in the Pennsylvania wilderness.
German pastries are very good, and Bavarian desserts are even more varied, much like those of their southern neighbor, Austria.
Fruit pastries are very common. Apples are used in pastries all year round, and in summer you can also enjoy cherries and plums.
Many German baked goods also feature almonds, hazelnuts, and other nuts. Popular cakes often pair perfectly with strong coffee.
If you'd like a rich-flavored sweet snack, try a jam-filled pastry. This dessert goes by different names in different regions, such as Berliner, Pfannkuchen, and Krapfen.
Curry is a dish made primarily with herbs and spices, served with meat or vegetables.
Curry dishes can be either “dry” or “wet,” depending on the amount of water used.
In the inland regions of northern India and Pakistan, yogurt is often used in curry; whereas in southern India and some other coastal regions of the subcontinent, coconut milk is often used.
Indonesia has 17,000 islands, so Indonesian cuisine is just a general term that encompasses a wide variety of regional cuisines across the country.
But without further qualification, the term often refers to food originating from the central and eastern regions of Java, the main island.

Today, Javanese cuisine can be found throughout the archipelago, with many dishes using simple seasonings, and the main seasonings favored by the Javanese are peanuts, chili, sugar (especially Javanese coconut sugar), and various spices.
Stirrups hang on both sides of the saddle and are used to support the rider's feet.
They provide the rider with better stability, but they also pose a safety risk because the rider's foot can get caught.
If a rider is thrown from the horse but one foot gets caught in the stirrup, the horse may drag them along while running. To minimize this risk, some preventive measures can be taken.
First, most riders wear heeled riding boots, and the soles are smooth and fairly narrow.
Second, some saddles—especially English saddles—are equipped with safety releases, so if a fallen rider is pulled backward, the stirrup leather will come off the saddle.
Cochamó Valley, Chile’s premier climbing destination, is known as the Yosemite (Yosemite) of South America, with all kinds of massive granite walls and cliffs.
From the mountain peaks, breathtakingly magnificent views unfold. Climbers from around the world continue to forge new routes on its seemingly endless cliffs that serve as climbing walls.
Alpine skiing includes regular skiing and snowboarding, and it is a very popular sport. In this sport, skis or a snowboard are attached to your feet as you glide down snow-covered terrain.
Skiing is the main travel activity for many ski enthusiasts, sometimes also called “ski fanatics,” who plan to spend their entire vacation skiing in one location.
The idea of skiing goes back a long way—cave paintings depicting skiers date back to 5000 BC!
As a sport, alpine skiing can be traced back at least to the 17th century, and in 1861 Norwegians founded the first recreational ski club in Australia.
Free ski touring: this activity is also called cross-country skiing, ski touring, or ski trekking.
This activity is related to alpine ski touring or mountaineering, but usually involves neither. Both of those activities take place on steep terrain and require stiffer skis and boots.
Think of a ski route as a similar hiking route.
When conditions are good, you can travel farther than on foot—but only in very rare cases can you achieve the same speed as cross-country skiing on a flat track without carrying a heavy pack.

Europe is a continent with a relatively small land area but many independent countries. Under normal circumstances, traveling through multiple countries means having to go through visa applications and passport checks many times.
However, in this respect, the Schengen Area is a bit like a single country.
As long as you are staying within this area, you can usually cross borders directly without going through passport control again.
Similarly, with a Schengen visa, you do not need to apply for a visa separately to each Schengen member state, saving time, money, and paperwork.
There is no uniform definition of which manufactured items are considered antiques. Some tax authorities define items over 100 years old as antiques.
This definition varies by region; in North America and elsewhere, the age threshold may be shorter than in Europe.
Handicrafts may be defined as antiques, even though they are much newer than comparable mass-produced items.
Reindeer herding is an important livelihood for the Sámi. The culture surrounding this industry is also very important to many people who make a living in other occupations.

However, even in the traditional sense, not all Sami were involved in large-scale reindeer herding; they made a living from fishing, hunting, and similar activities, and usually used reindeer as draft animals.
Today, many Sami people work in modern industries. Tourism is one of the main sources of income in Sápmi, that is, the Sami region.
Although the term “Gypsy” is widely used, especially among non-Roma people, it is generally considered offensive because it is associated with negative stereotypes and misconceptions about the Roma.
If the country you plan to visit is subject to a travel warning, your travel health insurance or trip cancellation insurance may be affected.
You can also consult advice from governments other than your own, but their advice is intended for their own citizens.
For example, U.S. citizens in the Middle East may face circumstances different from those of Europeans or Arabs.
Travel advisories can only provide a brief summary of the local situation.
Compared with the more detailed information provided elsewhere, the advice offered here is often rather rough, general, and overly simplistic.
Severe weather is a general term for any dangerous weather phenomenon; this kind of weather can cause destruction, serious social disruption, or loss of life.
Severe weather can occur anywhere in the world. There are many different types of severe weather, depending on geographic location, terrain, and atmospheric conditions.
High winds, hail, excessive precipitation, wildfires, as well as thunderstorms, tornadoes, waterspouts, and cyclones, are all manifestations and impacts of extreme weather.
Regional and seasonal severe weather phenomena include snowstorms, blizzards, ice storms, and dust storms.
Travelers are strongly advised to pay attention to the risk of severe weather at their location, as it may affect travel plans.
Anyone planning to travel to a country that may be considered a war zone should receive professional training.
Search online for “hostile environment course”; you may be able to find the addresses of local companies offering training.
This course will usually cover all the issues discussed here, often exploring them in greater depth through practical field experience.
The course training usually lasts 2 to 5 days and involves role-playing, first aid training, and sometimes weapons training.
Books and magazines about wilderness survival are common, but publications about survival in war zones are rare.
Travelers planning to undergo gender reassignment surgery abroad must carry valid identification documents on their return trip.
As for whether governments are willing to issue passports without a gender marker (X), or amend documents to match a person's desired name and gender, it varies from country to country.
The degree to which foreign governments recognize these documents also varies widely.
In the post-September 11, 2001 era, searches at security checkpoints also became more intrusive.
Transgender people who have not undergone surgery are forced to disclose their private information when passing through scanners, and their dignity cannot be guaranteed.
A rip current is a fast backflow of water in which waves quickly flow back from the beach into the sea, usually occurring near reefs or similar areas.
Underwater topography causes the return flow to concentrate in several deeper areas, where strong currents flowing toward deep water may form.
Most deaths are caused by trying to swim against the current and becoming exhausted; successfully swimming against the flow is highly unlikely.
Once you're out of the swift current, swimming back won't be that difficult.
Swim as hard as you can to a place where you won’t get trapped again, or wait for rescue (depending on your skills and whether you’ve been spotted).
The shock of returning home comes faster than culture shock (the honeymoon period is shorter), lasts longer, and can be more severe.
For travelers who adapt very easily to a new culture, it can sometimes be difficult to readapt to their native culture.
After living abroad for a while and returning home, you may find that you have already adapted to the new culture and shed some habits from your home culture.
When you first go abroad, people may be very patient and understanding with you, because they know that a traveler in a new country needs time to adjust.
People may not realize that travelers also need patience and understanding on the way back.
For children, the pyramids' sound and light show is one of the most interesting activities in the area.
In the darkness, you can see the pyramids, and before the show begins, you can see them standing there silently.
Normally, you can always hear the voices of tourists and street vendors here. Light and sound intertwine, as if telling a moving story.
On the backdrop is the Sphinx, which is also a storyteller of this long tale.
The scene is displayed on the pyramids, with different pyramids lighting up one by one.

The South Shetland Islands were discovered in 1819. Several countries claim sovereignty over the archipelago, which also has the largest number of bases, with sixteen bases in operation on the islands in 2020.
The archipelago is located 120 kilometers north of the Antarctic Peninsula. The largest island is King George Island, home to the settlement of “Villa Las Estrellas.”
Others include Livingston Island and Deception Island. There, a still-active volcanic crater was flooded, creating a spectacular natural harbor.
Ellsworth Land is located in the southern part of the peninsula, facing the Bellingshausen Sea.
Here, the mountain ranges of the peninsula merge into the plateau, then re-form into the 360-kilometer-long Ellsworth Mountains, which are split in two by the Minnesota Glacier.
The northern section, or the Sentinel Range, contains the Vinson Mountains, the highest mountain range in Antarctica, whose highest peak is Mount Vinson at 4,892 meters.
In remote areas beyond mobile signal coverage, a satellite phone may be your only option.
Generally speaking, a satellite phone cannot replace a mobile phone, because calls can only be made outdoors where the satellite signal is clear.
This service is often used in shipping, including yachts, as well as expeditions with remote data and voice needs.
Your local telephone service provider should be able to provide detailed information about connecting to this service.
For those planning how to spend their gap year, study travel is becoming an increasingly popular choice.
This is especially popular among high school graduates, who can take a year off before university without affecting their education.
In many cases, taking part in a gap year program abroad can significantly improve your chances of continuing your education after returning home.
Typically, participating in these educational programs requires paying tuition.
Finland is an exceptional haven for sailing, renowned as the “Land of a Thousand Lakes,” and its lakes and coastal archipelagos are also home to thousands of islands.
Among the archipelagos and lakes, a yacht is not a necessity.
The coastal archipelagos and the largest lakes are spacious enough for any yacht, but smaller boats and even kayaks can offer a different experience.
Boating is a national pastime in Finland, with one boat for every seven or eight people.
Norway, Sweden, and New Zealand have similar situations as well, but in other respects this is quite unique (for example, in the Netherlands, the figure is one boat for every forty people).
Most typical Baltic cruises spend a longer time in St. Petersburg, Russia.
This means you can have several full days to visit this historic city, then return to the ship at night to sleep.
If you go ashore only as part of a cruise excursion, no separate visa is required (regulations as of 2009).

Some cruise lines feature Berlin, Germany, in their promotional brochures as a highlight. As can be seen from the map above, Berlin is nowhere near the sea, and the cruise price does not include the cost of visiting this city.
For people of all ages and backgrounds, air travel can be a frightening experience, especially if they have never flown before or have had a painful experience.
This is nothing to be ashamed of: it is no different from the fear and aversion many people have toward other things.
For some people, to overcome the fear caused by the unknown or uncontrollable situations, it may help to understand how airplanes work and the procedures of flying.
Courier companies can earn high fees by delivering goods quickly. In many cases, time is critical for business documents, merchandise, or parts needed for emergency repairs.
On some routes, large companies have their own aircraft, but for other routes and smaller companies, that becomes a problem.
If they ship the goods by air, on some routes it may take days to unload and clear customs.
The fastest way is to ship it as checked baggage. Under airline regulations, baggage cannot be carried if the passenger does not board the flight, so you would need to be there in person.
The most obvious way to fly first class or business class is to spend a lot of money for the privilege (or, better yet, have your company pay for it).
However, this isn’t cheap: by rough estimates, a business-class ticket can cost 4 times as much as a regular economy ticket, and a first-class ticket can cost as much as 11 times more!
Generally speaking, if you're flying directly from A to B, there's absolutely no need to look for discounted business-class or first-class tickets.
Airlines know very well that some core passengers are willing to pay a high price for the privilege of fast and comfortable travel, so they charge accordingly.
The capital of Moldova is Chișinău. The local language is Romanian, but Russian is also widely spoken.
Moldova is a multiethnic republic that has long suffered from ethnic conflict.

In 1994, this conflict led to the establishment in eastern Moldova of the self-proclaimed Transnistrian Republic. The republic has its own government and currency, but it has not been recognized by any UN member state.
Although political negotiations failed, economic ties between these two regions of Moldova were restored.
The main religion in Moldova is Eastern Orthodoxy.
Izmir is Turkey’s third-largest city, with a population of about 3.7 million. It is Turkey’s second-largest port after Istanbul, and it is also a busy transportation hub.
Once the ancient city of Smyrna, it has now become a modern, developed, and bustling commercial center, surrounded by a vast bay and mountains.

Amid broad tree-lined boulevards, glass-fronted buildings, and modern shopping centers are scattered traditional red-tiled roofs, 18th-century markets, and ancient mosques and churches, yet the city feels more Mediterranean European than traditionally Turkish.
In the village of Haldarsvík, you can enjoy beautiful views of the nearby island of Eysturoy. The village has a distinctive octagonal church.
In the church cemetery, some graves are topped with interesting marble dove sculptures.
It's worth spending half an hour walking around this charming village.
To the north, you can easily reach the romantic and charming town of Sintra; Lord Byron praised it in his writings as a paradise, and it became known to foreigners as a result.
Scotturb public bus 403 runs regularly to Sintra (Sintra), stopping at Cabo da Roca.
You can also head north to visit the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Fatima, a holy site world-famous as the place where the Virgin Mary appeared.
Please remember that you are in fact visiting the site of a vast mass grave, a place of immeasurable significance to a great many people around the world.
And yet many men and women survived here, and are still alive today; and many more people had relatives who were murdered there, or were worked to death, Jewish and non-Jewish alike.
Please give this place the dignity, solemnity, and respect it deserves. Do not joke about the Holocaust or the Nazis.
Do not mark or graffiti the buildings, so as not to damage the site.
Barcelona's official languages are Catalan and Spanish. About half of the locals prefer to speak Catalan, and the vast majority understand Catalan. As for Spanish, almost everyone understands it.
However, most signs use only Catalan, because it is the first official language stipulated by law.
However, Spanish is also widely used in public transportation and other facilities.
Regular announcements in the metro are only in Catalan, but the automated system makes temporary announcements in multiple languages, including Spanish, English, French, Arabic, and Japanese.
Parisians are notorious for being self-centered, rude, and arrogant.

Although this is usually just an inaccurate stereotype, the best way to live in Paris is still to behave as properly as possible and come across as someone who is “bien élevé” (well brought up). This will make attending social events much easier.
If you show a little basic politeness, Parisians' rudeness will quickly vanish without a trace.
Plitvice Lakes National Park is densely forested, with beech, spruce, and fir as its main tree species, and is characterized by a mix of alpine and Mediterranean vegetation.
Thanks to the diversity of microclimates, soils, and elevations, the plant communities here are exceptionally rich in variety and are renowned worldwide.
This region is also home to various animals and birds.
There you can see rare animals such as the European brown bear, wolf, eagle, owl, lynx, wildcat, and grouse, as well as many more common species.
Women must wear skirts when visiting the monastery; the skirts must be knee-length, and the shoulders must also be covered.
Most monasteries will provide a wrap skirt for women who come unprepared, but if you bring your own—especially a brightly colored one—the monks at the entrance will give you a tight-lipped smile.
Likewise, men must also wear long pants that cover the knees.
You can also borrow one from the cloakroom at the entrance, but these clothes aren’t washed after every use, so these skirts might feel a little uncomfortable to wear. They’re all one size only!
The cuisine of Mallorca, like that of similar Mediterranean regions, is based mainly on bread, vegetables, and meat (especially pork), and uses olive oil exclusively.
Pa amb Oli is a simple dinner especially popular in summer: bread with olive oil, tomato, and whatever toppings are on hand, such as cheese, tuna, and so on.
All nouns, except “Sie” when it denotes the formal “you,” should begin with a capital letter, even in the middle of a sentence.
This is an important way to distinguish certain verbs from objects.
This can also make reading easier, although it complicates writing to some extent, because you need to determine whether a verb or adjective is being used in a nominalized form.
Italian pronunciation is relatively easy, because the pronunciation of most words matches their spelling exactly.
The main letters to pay attention to are c and g, because their pronunciation changes depending on the vowels that follow.
Additionally, make sure to pronounce r and rr differently: “caro” means “dear,” while “carro” means “two-wheeled chariot.”
Persian grammar is for the most part relatively simple and follows regular patterns.
Therefore, reading this introductory grammar book can help you master more Persian grammar and better understand phrases.
There is no doubt that if you speak a Romance language, learning Portuguese will be much easier.
However, people who know a little Spanish may rush to the conclusion that Portuguese is very close to Spanish and does not need to be learned separately.
Nowadays, most pre-modern observatories have already been phased out and preserved only as museums or educational sites.
Because light pollution was not as severe in their heyday as it is now, observatories were usually built in urban areas or on campuses, making them easier to reach than modern observatories.
Most modern research telescopes are giant facilities located in remote areas with favorable atmospheric conditions.
Cherry blossom viewing, also known as “hanami,” has been a Japanese custom since the 8th century.
This concept originated in China, and plum blossoms are a type of flower much loved by the Chinese people.
In Japan, the earliest cherry blossom parties were held by the emperor for himself and other noble members of the imperial family at the Imperial Palace.
Plants are at their best in their natural environment. So resist the temptation not to pick them, even if it’s “just one plant.”
If you're visiting a meticulously maintained garden, collecting “specimens” will also get you kicked out, with no room for negotiation.
Generally speaking, Singapore is a very safe place, and it's also easy to get around. Once you arrive, you can buy almost anything.

But since it lies in the "high tropics," a few degrees north of the equator, you will need to cope with both high temperatures (year-round) and strong sunlight (when the weather is clear, which is relatively rare).
Some buses also head north to Hebron, the traditional burial site of the biblical patriarchs Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and their wives.
Please make sure the bus you plan to take actually goes into Hebron, and not just to the nearby Jewish settlement of Kiryat Arba.
Inland waterways can make a great theme for a vacation.
For example, visit castles in the Loire Valley and the Rhine Valley, take a cruise to explore the interesting cities along the Danube, or travel by boat along the Erie Canal.
They also clearly mark routes for popular hiking and cycling trails.
Christmas is one of the most important festivals in Christianity, established to celebrate the birth of Christ.
topic: Reason to travel/Christmas and New Year

text: Many traditions associated with this holiday have also been embraced by non-believers in Christian countries and by non-Christians around the world.
topic: Reason to travel/Christmas and New Year

text: There's a tradition of staying awake somewhere outdoors on Easter night to watch the sunrise.
Of course, this tradition has its Christian theological explanation, but it most likely existed even before the birth of Christ as a ritual to celebrate spring and pray for a good harvest.

More traditional churches often hold an Easter Vigil on Saturday night of Easter weekend, and the congregation usually pours into the celebration at the stroke of midnight, celebrating the resurrection of Christ.
At first, animals reached these islands by swimming, flying, or drifting.
Because of their distance from the mainland, mammals could not make the long journey there, making giant tortoises the primary herbivores of the Colón Islands.
After humans arrived in the Galápagos Islands, they introduced many mammals, including goats, horses, cattle, rats, cats, and dogs.
If you travel to the Arctic or Antarctic regions in winter, you can experience the polar night, a period during which the sun does not rise above the horizon.
This provides an excellent opportunity to see the Northern Lights, because the sky will be more or less continuously dark all day long.
These areas are sparsely populated, so light pollution is generally not a problem, and you can also enjoy a brilliant starry sky.
Compared with the work culture Westerners are used to, Japanese work culture has a more pronounced hierarchy and is more formal.
Suits are the standard business attire, and colleagues address one another by their last names or job titles.
A harmonious workplace atmosphere is crucial, emphasizing team effort rather than praising individual achievements.
Employees must obtain approval from their superiors before making any decisions and must unconditionally obey their superiors' instructions.
